codeReview.summary: The implementation successfully integrates the rechecker module into workspace_validator.rs for fresh-container replay with auto-fix capabilities. All required functionality is implemented: the WorkspaceValidator struct has a rechecker field, the with_rechecker_config() constructor exists, fresh_container_revalidation() uses rechecker for both base and patched container install failures, and install_commands are updated after successful fixes. The code properly handles all RecheckResult variants (Fixed, Incorrigible, and other cases) with appropriate logging and cleanup. 8 unit tests were added (exceeding the required 7), all passing.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker followed all documented conventions from AGENTS.md: used proper error handling with thiserror, added tracing logs (not println), followed async Send + Sync patterns with Arc, and respected the semaphore-based concurrency approach.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/swe/workspace_validator.rs:824-880 - proper tracing::warn!, tracing::info!, tracing::error! usage; proper match handling for all RecheckResult variants; sandbox cleanup with destroy().await in all branches

sharedStateObservations.observation: The worker followed the rust-dev-worker skill procedure correctly: read existing code first, designed the integration to match existing patterns, wrote tests, implemented, and verified. The handoff.skillFeedback.followedProcedure is true with no deviations.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: handoff file shows followedProcedure: true, deviations: []; tests were added before implementation as per TDD approach; integration matches existing WorkspaceValidator::new pattern

sharedStateObservations.observation: No new services or commands need to be added to services.yaml. The rechecker integration is purely internal to the WorkspaceValidator and doesn't require external services.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Implementation only adds internal module integration; no CLI commands or external dependencies added

sharedStateObservations.observation: The rechecker integration correctly handles the fresh-container replay pattern that was already documented in workspace_validator.rs module docs. The worker properly understood that install failures in both base AND patched containers should trigger rechecker fixes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: src/swe/workspace_validator.rs:824-880 (base container) and src/swe/workspace_validator.rs:937-992 (patched container) both have identical rechecker integration logic

summary: Feature validator-rechecker-integration is fully implemented and verified. The rechecker is properly integrated into WorkspaceValidator with the rechecker field, with_rechecker_config() constructor, and fresh_container_revalidation() calling rechecker.fix_install() on both base and patched container failures. All 19 workspace_validator tests pass (including 8 rechecker integration tests, exceeding the required 7). The implementation fulfills VAL-HARNESS-RETRY-002 as specified in the feature requirements. No issues found.
